content:
- Read Ralph startup state: previous progress was empty, current task points to story-21 task 02 about massively improving image build speed with Docker layer/build cache reuse.
- Read required skills improve-code-boundaries and tdd to align with mandatory planning/execution workflow.
- Next: inspect the task file, determine whether it is in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE mode, then either create/update a plan or execute it.

- Read the task file header and acceptance criteria for story-21 task 02: it is a large workflow/cache refactor centered on Docker and GitHub Actions image build speed.
- Need to determine whether the task contains a TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE marker before taking action, per Ralph workflow.
- Next: inspect the rest of the task file and then either create a plan in the required plans directory or begin TDD execution.

- Confirmed the current implementation shape: publish-images workflow has validate + publish jobs but no explicit build cache plumbing; all three image Dockerfiles currently copy full source trees before dependency/build steps, which destroys cache reuse on unrelated edits.
- Confirmed the repo already has contract-test owners for the workflow and each image Dockerfile, so the plan can extend those boundaries instead of creating duplicate test harnesses.
- Next: write a concrete plan file in the task plan directory, mark it TO BE VERIFIED, add the plan path to the task file, and stop.

- Created the plan file for story-21 task 02 at .ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/02-task-massively-improve-image-build-speed-with-docker-layer-and-build-cache-reuse_plans/2026-04-20-image-build-cache-speed-plan.md.
- Updated the task markdown to include the <plan> pointer and left the plan ending in TO BE VERIFIED, because the task had no execution marker and the workflow requires planning first.
- Planned execution around TDD slices and improve-code-boundaries: new shared build-target contract, shared Rust cache contract helper, Dockerfile cache-layer refactors, workflow host/build cache plumbing, parallel publish topology, and explicit arm64/cargo-chef decisions.
- Next: after reviewing the plan and confirming the design is right, replace the end marker with NOW EXECUTE and then execute the slices in red/green order.

- Re-read previous Ralph progress and loaded the mandatory improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions for this turn.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt to resolve the active task path for story-21 task 02.
- Next: inspect the task markdown and linked plan state to determine whether this is a TO BE VERIFIED stop or a NOW EXECUTE pass.

- Read the task markdown and existing plan file; the plan still ends in TO BE VERIFIED, so execution is not yet allowed.
- Confirmed the next decision is whether the plan design is sound enough to flip to NOW EXECUTE, which requires checking the current workflow and contract seams.
- Next: inspect the workflow YAML and existing contract helpers for image publishing, then either tighten the plan or mark it ready for execution.

- Sanity-checked the existing workflow contract seam: publish-images behavior is already centralized in GithubWorkflowContract, so the plan can extend one honest parser boundary instead of inventing a second one.
- Next I am checking the Docker contract helpers and Dockerfiles to confirm the shared Rust cache helper and per-image cache-boundary assertions are the right design before flipping the plan marker.

- Verified the existing workflow and Docker/test boundaries against the plan: GithubWorkflowContract is the right single workflow owner, the two Rust image contract helpers do duplicate cache-shape knowledge, and the current Dockerfiles are still cache-hostile exactly as described by the plan.
- Decided the plan is sound enough to execute without redesign; flipped `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/02-task-massively-improve-image-build-speed-with-docker-layer-and-build-cache-reuse_plans/2026-04-20-image-build-cache-speed-plan.md` from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: on the execution turn, read the tdd skill again, follow the plan in strict red/green slices, keep the improve-code-boundaries cleanup explicit, and only finish after make check/lint/test pass.

- Re-read persisted progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions at startup.
- Confirmed the active task is story-21 task 02 and prior progress indicates the plan was already flipped to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: read the task markdown and linked plan in full, then execute the plan in red/green slices and keep appending progress during the work.

- Read the task markdown and NOW EXECUTE plan; the repository is still at the pre-refactor state, so the work is not already implemented.
- Confirmed the main gaps: publish-images workflow is still sequential and uncached, Dockerfiles still copy full source before dependency work, and the new contract helper files from the plan do not exist.
- Next: inspect the existing image/workflow contract tests, then start the first red/green slice by adding failing workflow coverage for shared build-target cache ownership.

- Added the first red contract expectations for validation cache reuse, shared publish topology/buildkit cache reuse, and cache-friendly Rust/Go Dockerfile layer boundaries.
- The implementation is still missing, so the next step is to run targeted tests and use the failures to drive the workflow/helper/Dockerfile refactor.
- Next: inspect the compile/test failures, then add the new contract owners and minimal implementation needed to turn those red slices green.

- Implemented the main refactor surface: new shared image build target contract, shared Rust workspace Docker cache contract, cache-aware Rust/Go Dockerfiles, and a parallelized publish workflow with manifest aggregation.
- Next: fix any compile or contract mistakes from the first implementation pass, then run the targeted contract tests again before broader repo validation.

- The first red/green slices are now green: targeted workflow cache assertions and the new Rust/Go Dockerfile cache-boundary assertions all pass.
- Next: run the full ci/image contract suites, fix any remaining workflow expectation drift, then move to repo-wide make check/lint/test gates.

- Fixed the clippy/module-boundary regressions from the first full gate run by flattening ImageBuildTargetSpec construction and moving the shared Rust cache helper import inside the support files that actually own it.
- `make check` passed and `make lint` passed after those fixes.
- Next: wait for `make test`, then only if it passes update the task file, switch task, commit, and push.

- `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after the cache/workflow refactor.
- Next: update the task markdown with the actual outcome and pass state, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then commit and push the finished task.
